title: Pharmacy Dispensing Audit
spl:
index=clinical sourcetype="pharmacy:dispense" dea_schedule IN ("2","3","4")
| search override_flag="true" OR fill_hour<6 OR fill_hour>22
| stats count, sum(quantity) as units by drug_name, pharmacist_id, patient_hash
| sort -units
description: Tracks controlled substance dispensing overrides and after-hours fills for diversion programs.
value: Tracks controlled substance dispensing overrides and after-hours fills for diversion programs.
implementation: Align with state PDMP reporting; peer review for high override rates.
visualization: Table (overrides), Bar chart (by drug), Heatmap (pharmacist × shift).
knownFalsePositives: Pharmacy-dispense anomalies from 340B programs, off-hours compounding, and after-hours retail volumes; use formulary and shift context.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Pharmacy system (Pyxis, Epic Willow).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="pharmacy:dispense"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Align with state PDMP reporting; peer review for high override rates.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=clinical sourcetype="pharmacy:dispense" dea_schedule IN ("2","3","4")
| search override_flag="true" OR fill_hour<6 OR fill_hour>22
| stats count, sum(quantity) as units by drug_name, pharmacist_id, patient_hash
| sort -units
```

Understanding this SPL

**Pharmacy Dispensing Audit** — Tracks controlled substance dispensing overrides and after-hours fills for diversion programs.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="pharmacy:dispense"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Pharmacy system (Pyxis, Epic Willow). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: clinical; **sourcetype**: pharmacy:dispense. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=clinical, sourcetype="pharmacy:dispense". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by drug_name, pharmacist_id, patient_hash** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overrides), Bar chart (by drug), Heatmap (pharmacist × shift).
grandmaExplanation: In Healthcare, we pay close attention to "Pharmacy Dispensing Audit" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
